# Understudy

> Understudy runs swarms of behavior-grounded AI agents through a company's real product flows to predict which version of an experiment wins, who abandons, and why, before a single real user sees it.

## What it is

Understudy is a simulated-user experimentation platform. Instead of waiting weeks for a live A/B test to reach significance, teams run a swarm of AI agents, each grounded in real behavioral data, through their actual product flow (checkout, onboarding, pricing page, etc.). The swarm predicts which variant will win, estimates the completion/abandonment rate for each, and explains the drop-off reasons.

## How it works

- **Grounded agents**: each agent's behavior is conditioned on real demographic and behavioral data, not generic role-play.
- **Real flows**: agents navigate the company's actual staging site or prototype, not a mock.
- **Prediction**: the swarm outputs which variant wins, per-segment completion rates, and the specific friction that causes abandonment.
- **Validation**: the method is backtested on public randomized experiments with known outcomes. On a real 64,000-customer email experiment, grounded agents picked the winning treatment per customer segment with 94% accuracy (p = 0.0003) and predicted effect sizes correlating +0.70 with the real ones; scrambling the behavioral grounding collapsed accuracy to 35% and inverted the correlation to -0.36. The signal comes from the grounding, not chance or memorization. Understudy also publishes where the method fails (aesthetic headline prediction is at chance under paraphrase controls).

## Who it's for

Growth, product, and CRO (conversion rate optimization) teams who want to de-risk experiments and prioritize the changes most likely to win before committing engineering and live traffic.

## Key facts

- Category: simulated-user testing / experimentation / conversion optimization.
- Differentiator: predictions are grounded in real behavior and validated by blind backtests, not vibes.
- Status: early access.

## Links

- Home and early access: https://understudy.com
